ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region where the customer is located */,
  "tenure" INTEGER /* Tenure of the customer with the company */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer's address */,
  "income" REAL /* Income level of the customer */,
  "education" TEXT /* Education level of the customer */,
  "retirement_status" TEXT /* Retirement status of the customer */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the customer */,
  "voice_service" TEXT /* Voice service usage */,
  "internet_service" TEXT /* Internet service usage */,
  "forwarding_service" TEXT /* Forwarding service usage */,
  "customer_category" TEXT /* Customer category (e.g., Basic, Plus, Total) */,
  "churn_status" TEXT /* Churn status of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "preferred_contact_method" TEXT /* Preferred method of contact (e.g., email, phone) */,
  "account_creation_date" TEXT /* Date the customer account was created */,
  "last_interaction_date" TEXT /* Date of the last interaction with the customer */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Loyalty points accumulated by the customer */,
  "referral_code" TEXT /* Referral code if the customer was referred by another customer */,
  "referring_customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who referred this customer */,
  "language_preference" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customers_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "street_address" TEXT /* Street address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer resides */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer resides */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the customer's address */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the customer resides */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the customer's address for geolocation */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the customer's address for geolocation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "services" (
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer using the service */,
  "service_type" TEXT /* Type of service (e.g., voice, internet, forwarding) */,
  "activation_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was activated */,
  "service_status" TEXT /* Status of the service (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "service_plan" TEXT /* Specific plan of the service (e.g., Basic, Plus, Total) */,
  "service_price" REAL /* Price of the service */,
  "last_renewal_date" TEXT /* Date the service was last renewed */,
  "next_renewal_date" TEXT /* Date the service is due for renewal */,
  "service_discount" REAL /* Any discounts applied to the service */,
  "auto_renew" TEXT /* Whether the service is set to auto-renew (Yes/No) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_services_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_interactions" (
  "interaction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer interaction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_type" TEXT /* Type of interaction (e.g., call, email, chat) */,
  "interaction_date" TEXT /* Date when the interaction occurred */,
  "interaction_details" TEXT /* Details of the interaction */,
  "interaction_channel" TEXT /* Channel of interaction (e.g., call, email, chat) */,
  "interaction_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the interaction */,
  "interaction_outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the interaction (e.g., resolved, pending) */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer service agent involved in the interaction */,
  "interaction_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the interaction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("interaction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_interactions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "billing" (
  "billing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each billing record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer associated with the billing record */,
  "invoice_date" TEXT /* Date when the invoice was generated */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the payment */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Status of the payment (e.g., paid, unpaid) */,
  "total_amount" REAL /* Total amount due for the invoice */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used (e.g., credit card, bank transfer) */,
  "payment_reference" TEXT /* Reference number for the payment */,
  "late_fee" REAL /* Any late fees applied to the invoice */,
  "invoice_number" TEXT /* Invoice number */,
  "invoice_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the invoice */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("billing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_billing_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_issues" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service issue */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer reporting the issue */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service with the issue */,
  "issue_description" TEXT /* Description of the service issue */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date when the issue was reported */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the issue resolution */,
  "issue_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the issue (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date the issue was resolved */,
  "resolution_time" INTEGER /* Time taken to resolve the issue */,
  "agent_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer service agent who resolved the issue */,
  "resolution_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_issues_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_issues_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotions" (
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion */,
  "promotion_name" TEXT /* Name of the promotion */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the promotion */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the promotion */,
  "promotion_details" TEXT /* Details of the promotion */,
  "promotion_type" TEXT /* Type of promotion (e.g., discount, free service) */,
  "target_customer_segment" TEXT /* Segment of customers targeted by the promotion */,
  "promotion_code" TEXT /* Unique code for the promotion */,
  "promotion_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the promotion is offered (e.g., email, SMS) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("promotion_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "promotion_redemptions" (
  "redemption_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each promotion redemption */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer redeeming the promotion */,
  "promotion_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the promotion redeemed */,
  "redemption_date" TEXT /* Date when the promotion was redeemed */,
  "redemption_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the promotion was redeemed (e.g., online, in-store) */,
  "redemption_value" REAL /* Value of the redeemed promotion */,
  "redemption_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the redemption */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("redemption_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotion_redemptions_promotion_id FOREIGN KEY ("promotion_id") REFERENCES promotions ("promotion_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_promotion_redemptions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "churn_predictions" (
  "prediction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each churn prediction */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer for whom the prediction is made */,
  "prediction_date" TEXT /* Date when the prediction was made */,
  "churn_probability" REAL /* Probability of the customer churning */,
  "recommended_action" TEXT /* Recommended action to prevent churn */,
  "churn_reasons" TEXT /* Reasons for the churn prediction */,
  "churn_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the churn risk (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "churn_prevention_cost" REAL /* Estimated cost of preventing churn */,
  "churn_prevention_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the churn prevention actions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prediction_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_churn_predictions_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service usage record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the customer using the service */,
  "service_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the service being used */,
  "usage_date" TEXT /* Date when the service was used */,
  "usage_duration" INTEGER /* Duration of service usage */,
  "data_usage" REAL /* Data usage for internet services */,
  "usage_type" TEXT /* Type of usage (e.g., voice, data) */,
  "usage_price" REAL /* Price of the usage */,
  "usage_discount" REAL /* Any discounts applied to the usage */,
  "usage_notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the usage */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_usage_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_service_usage_service_id FOREIGN KEY ("service_id") REFERENCES services ("service_id")
)
